description: This schematic illustrates the TRIP (Transformation-Induced Plasticity) effect as a deformation mechanism in medium-Mn steels, showing the progressive transformation of retained austenite into martensite with increasing strain (ε). The left panel depicts the initial microstructure consisting of α-ferrite and two types of austenite: γ_c (austenite undergoing direct TRIP to become α') and γ_f (austenite undergoing dislocation glide followed by TRIP), with red arrows indicating Mn partitioning from ferrite to austenite. The middle panel shows that under increasing strain, γ_c has transformed into lath-type α'-martensite while dislocations (⊥ symbols) accumulate in γ_f. The right panel demonstrates further deformation where γ_f also begins transforming to martensite. This figure elucidates how deformation-induced martensite forms inside austenite grains and supports the conclusion that austenite stability is governed by Mn enrichment and strain levels, explaining the enhanced strength-ductility synergy in intercritically annealed low-density steels.
